verses.text: The work finished, Solomon brings in all the treasures David had dedicated and stores them in the house of God. The father's gifts join the son's completed work. God's house is built across generations, each adding to the labour of the last.

Solomon gathers all Israel to bring up the ark, and they offer sacrifices beyond number. The whole nation assembles around God's presence. The climax of all the building is not the gold but the coming of the LORD to dwell among His people.

Solomon gathers all Israel to bring up the ark, and they offer sacrifices beyond number. The whole nation assembles around God's presence. The climax of all the building is not the gold but the coming of the LORD to dwell among His people.

The priests set the ark in the most holy place beneath the cherubim's wings; in it are only the two tables of the law from Horeb. God's word lies at the heart of His dwelling. His presence and His commandments belong together at the centre of worship.

The priests set the ark in the most holy place beneath the cherubim's wings; in it are only the two tables of the law from Horeb. God's word lies at the heart of His dwelling. His presence and His commandments belong together at the centre of worship.

As the singers and trumpeters praise 'as one,' singing, 'for he is good; for his mercy endureth for ever,' a cloud fills the house, so the priests cannot stand to minister, for 'the glory of the LORD had filled the house.' United, whole-hearted praise of His goodness draws down His glory.
